seoTitle: How to Create Reports in Airtable
metaDescription: Build visual reports in Airtable using grouped grid views and the Interface Designer, with charts, rollup widgets, and shareable dashboards.
intro: Turning raw Airtable records into a readable report means combining grouping and filtering with visual summary widgets. This guide covers structuring your data in the grid view and building an interactive report page with the Interface Designer.

steps.heading: Group and summarize your grid
steps.body: Use grid view grouping, sorting, and filtering to structure your raw reporting data first.

steps.heading: Open the Interface Designer
steps.body: Navigate to the Interface Designer hub and create a new report page.

steps.heading: Choose your data source and layout
steps.body: Select your core data source tables and choose visual chart layouts for your report.

steps.heading: Add summary widgets
steps.body: Drag rollup metric widgets onto the page to display sums, averages, or percentages, and add progress bars or number callouts for key indicators.

steps.heading: Apply filters and publish
steps.body: Add filters so viewers can toggle by month or department, customize color formatting to highlight goals, and click Publish when the report is ready.

faq.question: Do I need a specific Airtable plan to use the Interface Designer?
faq.answer: The Interface Designer is available on select Airtable plans, so check your current subscription tier if the option isn't visible.

faq.question: Can viewers interact with the report, or is it static?
faq.answer: Published interfaces can include interactive filters, letting viewers toggle the report by criteria like month or department without editing the underlying data.

faq.question: How are rollup metrics calculated in a report widget?
faq.answer: Rollup widgets automatically calculate values like sums, averages, or percentages based on the records in your connected data source.

faq.question: Can I reuse a report template for a different base?
faq.answer: You can save your interface layout as a template and adapt the data source connections when building a similar report elsewhere.
